headline: Trafficking victims receive only RM4.8 million support annually
context: Government allocation for trafficking victim support totalled RM4.8 million in 2025, serving approximately 600 identified victims nationwide.

cards.big: Only RM4.8 million allocated to support 600 trafficking victims in 2025
cards.sub: Per-victim spending of RM8,000 covers shelter but little rehabilitation

cards.big: Anti-Trafficking in Persons Act 2007 Section 44 mandates government shelter provision
cards.sub: Victims held in shelter facilities with restricted movement akin to detention

cards.big: US TIP Report criticised Malaysia's victim-centred approach as insufficient in 2024
cards.sub: Victims treated as witnesses rather than rights-holders in legal process

cards.big: Average shelter stay of 14 months with no work rights or freedom of movement
cards.sub: Trafficking survivors describe shelter conditions as second captivity

cards.big: Sheltering victims without freedom or support is not rescue, it is relocation of confinement
Tenaganita proposed community-based alternatives to shelter detention in 2024
